preamble: The insertion of a voiced alveolar affricate [dz] where no sound was present is a case of prothesis or fortition. This often occurs at the beginning of words to provide a consonantal onset.
sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
